---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 7: Process-Structure-Property Modeling
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Process-Structure-Property Modeling
- Unit scope and role.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Connect process parameters to properties via structure.

## 03. Recap from Unit 6
- Segmentation-derived feature bridge.

## 04. PSP paradigm overview
- Causal chain in materials systems.

## 05. Process variable taxonomy
- Control knobs and constraints.

## 06. Structure descriptor taxonomy
- Microstructure representation choices.

## 07. Property target taxonomy
- Mechanical/functional outputs.

## 08. Data fusion challenge
- Multi-source integration.

## 09. Feature engineering pipeline
- Process + structure + context.

## 10. Temporal and spatial scales
- Mismatch handling.

## 11. Baseline PSP model forms
- Linear, tree, neural options.

## 12. Mediator perspective
- Structure as intermediate variable.

## 13. Hierarchical modeling intuition
- Multi-level dependencies.

## 14. Multi-task learning hint
- Joint property prediction.

## 15. Physics-informed constraints
- Preserve plausible behavior.

## 16. Causal confounding pitfalls
- Spurious process-property links.

## 17. Split strategy for PSP data
- Batch-aware and temporal splits.

## 18. Distribution shift in process windows
- Extrapolation risk.

## 19. Metric selection
- Accuracy + calibration + utility.

## 20. Uncertainty propagation
- Process to property confidence chain.

## 21. Sensitivity analysis
- Which parameters matter most.

## 22. Interaction effects
- Nonlinear coupling patterns.

## 23. Counterfactual process analysis
- What-if parameter changes.

## 24. Optimization readiness checks
- Trust before control.

## 25. Data sparsity strategies
- Transfer and surrogate ideas.

## 26. Missing-data handling
- Robustness to incomplete metadata.

## 27. Feature leakage traps
- Hidden proxies in lab logs.

## 28. Reproducibility standards
- Versioned pipelines.

## 29. Visualization patterns
- Process maps and response surfaces.

## 30. Case sketch: heat treatment to hardness
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: additive manufacturing
- Parameter-microstructure links.

## 32. Case sketch: spectroscopy-informed PSP
- Multimodal fusion scaffold.

## 33. Failure mode #1
- Model learns batch artifacts.

## 34. Failure mode #2
- Ignoring mediator structure.

## 35. Failure mode #3
- Overconfident extrapolation.

## 36. Mitigation checklist
- Practical guardrails.

## 37. Human-in-the-loop deployment
- Expert validation checkpoints.

## 38. Bridge to surrogate optimization
- Unit 8 transition.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: dataset assembly
- Build process-structure-property table.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: baseline model
- Train and evaluate first PSP model.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: uncertainty analysis
- Prediction intervals and calibration.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: sensitivity plots
- Rank influential process variables.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: counterfactuals
- Simulate actionable changes.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: report
- Summarize findings and caveats.

## 45. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 46. Summary table
- Inputs, mediators, outputs, risks.

## 47. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 48. Reading assignment
- Prepare surrogate optimization unit.

## 49. Transition preview
- Optimization and control loop.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
